questions.question: Define a cuboid and write its surface area and volume formulas.
questions.answer: A cuboid is a solid having six rectangular faces. The total surface area of a cuboid is 2(lb + bh + hl) and the volume is l × b × h.

questions.question: Define a cube and write its surface area and volume formulas.
questions.answer: A cube is a cuboid in which all edges are equal. The total surface area is 6a² and the volume is a³.

questions.question: Explain curved surface area of a cylinder with formula.
questions.answer: The curved surface area of a cylinder is the area of its curved portion and is given by 2πrh.

questions.question: Explain the concept of total surface area.
questions.answer: Total surface area is the sum of the areas of all surfaces of a solid, including curved and flat surfaces.

questions.question: Define a cone and explain its curved surface area.
questions.answer: A cone is a solid with a circular base and a vertex. Its curved surface area is πrl.

questions.question: Explain the volume of a cylinder.
questions.answer: The volume of a cylinder is the space occupied by it and is given by πr²h.

questions.question: Define a hemisphere and write its surface area formula.
questions.answer: A hemisphere is half of a sphere. Its curved surface area is 2πr².

questions.question: Explain the volume of a cone.
questions.answer: The volume of a cone is one-third the volume of a cylinder with the same base and height, given by (1/3)πr²h.

questions.question: Explain slant height of a cone.
questions.answer: Slant height is the distance from the vertex to any point on the circumference of the base and is given by √(r² + h²).

questions.question: Explain the units of surface area and volume.
questions.answer: Surface area is measured in square units like cm² and volume is measured in cubic units like cm³.

questions.question: Explain the curved surface area of a sphere.
questions.answer: The curved surface area of a sphere is the total area of its curved surface and is given by 4πr².

questions.question: Explain the volume of a hemisphere.
questions.answer: The volume of a hemisphere is half the volume of a sphere and is given by (2/3)πr³.

questions.question: Explain why π is used in surface area formulas.
questions.answer: π is used because circular surfaces are involved in curved solids.

questions.question: Explain the structure of a medicine capsule.
questions.answer: A medicine capsule consists of a cylinder with two hemispherical ends.

questions.question: Explain the concept of capacity.
questions.answer: Capacity is the amount of liquid that a container can hold.

questions.question: Explain why CSA is used to find paint required.
questions.answer: Paint is applied only on exposed curved surfaces, so CSA is used.

questions.question: Explain why volume is measured in cubic units.
questions.answer: Volume measures three-dimensional space, so cubic units are used.

questions.question: Explain the shape of a water tank.
questions.answer: Water tanks are generally cylindrical because they store large volume efficiently.

questions.question: Explain why hemisphere is half of a sphere.
questions.answer: A hemisphere is obtained by cutting a sphere into two equal parts.

questions.question: Explain the use of curved surfaces in daily life.
questions.answer: Curved surfaces provide strength, reduce stress and improve capacity.

questions.question: Explain the difference between CSA and TSA.
questions.answer: CSA includes only curved surfaces, while TSA includes all surfaces.

questions.question: Explain why a cube has equal faces.
questions.answer: All edges of a cube are equal, hence all faces are equal squares.

questions.question: Explain why sphere has no edges.
questions.answer: A sphere has a completely curved surface with no flat faces.

questions.question: Explain the role of height in volume calculation.
questions.answer: Height determines the vertical space occupied by a solid.

questions.question: Explain the importance of surface area in daily life.
questions.answer: Surface area helps in calculating paint, wrapping, insulation and heat loss.
